title: Container port mapping issue
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I want to use podman restful api to start image and implement port mapping.After I create a container, I use the command "podman ps -a" to see info of the container.It shows '0.0.0.0:4000->3000/tcp'.But I can't access this port in the brower.How should I do?
If I use the command 'podman run -p 0.0.0.0:4000:3000 rtw:latest' , it work well. My docker image is  rtw:latest and it has no problem.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.create a container, the api is http://127.0.0.1:8888/v3.0.0/libpod/containers/create the payload are:
```
{
"image":"docker.io/wzpoo/rtw:latest",
"portmappings:[
{"container_port":3000,
"host_ip":"0.0.0.0",
"host_port":4000,
"protocol":"tcp",
"range":1}
],
"user":"user",
"remove":true,
"command":["top"]
}
```
2.the response code is 201 and I decode the container id, then I use http://127.0.0.1:8888/v3.0.0/libpod/containers/{name}/start to start this container

**Describe the results you received:**
In the brower, I input 'http://127.0.0.1:4000', it show error. It means that port mapping failed.

**Describe the results you expected:**
I can access the mapping port in the brower

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
 version: v3.0.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

comments.body: It looks like you are overwriting the container command in your json payload. Remove the `"command":["top"]` part and it should work.

> It looks like you are overwriting the container command in your json payload. Remove the `"command":["top"]` part and it should work.

remove the "command": ["top"], the issue also exist.
Can you provide the output of podman inspect on the container.

> Can you provide the output of podman inspect on the container.

@Luap99  .In the podman reference, it said that if I want to edit 'portmapping' parameter ,I also should to edit parameter 'netns'.'netns' has two parameter: 'nsmode' and 'value',but I don't know how to write it. So I don't input this parameter. Is the question here? If there is problem, can you tell me how to write parameter 'netns'? I want to use mode 'bridge'.
I do not think you have to set the nsmode. The default one is bridge and it shows bridge in your inspect output.

My system is ubuntu18.04, so I use this podman version. If I use the command 'podman run -p 0.0.0.0:3000:10000 docker.io/wzpoo/rtw:latest, it work well. I want to use the podman api achieve the same result.
Compare the podman inspect output from a container created via api and cli and check if you see any difference. Also start the podman service with --log-level debug and look for errors.
@wzpoo  Were you able to make it work?

> @wzpoo Were you able to make it work?

Yes, I take your suggestion and try a new version '3.2.3'. It works.
